def: s.m. pl. (of رحم rěḥm), Wombs, matrices; relations by the mother's side, uterine kindred.
s.m. = اڐهانا aḍhānā, and اڙانا aṛānā, q.v. s.m. One of the thirty-six Rāginīs that are played or sung during the night only. (Also written aṛhānā and aṛānā).
s.m. A Buddha; a Jain saint; a holy person (a follower of Buddha).
s.f. A species of pulse, Cajanus Indicus: (Syn. توأر tūʼar).
s.f. A species of pulse, Cajanus Indicus: (Syn. توأر tūʼar).
= ارهر arhar, q.v. s.f. A species of pulse, Cajanus Indicus: (Syn. توأر tūʼar).

s.m. f. (?), A well-wheel, the (so-called) Persian wheel (a string of earthen pots attached to a revolving wheel over a well, which go down empty and come up full and tilt the water into a trough: — The commoner form of the word is رهٿ rahaṭ).

s.f. Agency, mercantile correspondence; the business of weighing, selling, or holding in charge the merchandise of dealers; also the remuneration for this service or agency; sale by commission; commission; brokerage.
s.m. Weeds; tares; oats.
s.m. A day-labourer.
s.m. Enemy, foe; a wheel
subs: s.m. Host or army of enemies, or of the enemy
s.m. prop. n. Destroyer or subduer of foes.

def: s.f. (Braj) A woman's female friend; dear, darling; a ridge or mound of earth crossing ditches, dividing fields, &c.; a dike; bank of a river.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀭𑀸 (ārā) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-), from Sanskrit आरा॑ (ā́rā).

def: s.m. Protector, supporter; backer; partner; one of a side (in a game).
s.f. = آري ārī (dim. of ārā), q.v. s.m. Enemy, foe; a wheel
subs: s.m. Host or army of enemies, or of the enemy
s.m. prop. n. Destroyer or subduer of foes.

def: s.f. A kind of musical mode; a tune (cf. aṛānā).
s.f. A small saw; a shoe-maker's awl.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀭𑀸 (ārā) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-), from Sanskrit आरा॑ (ā́rā).

def: s.f. Difficulty, hardship, trouble; need, poverty, distress, adversity, misfortune; exigency, critical situation
subs: s.f. Difficulties, troubles, cares, concerns, responsibilities; whatever betides; misfortunes, afflictions.

def: = ار ari, q.v. s.m. Enemy, foe; a wheel
subs: s.m. Host or army of enemies, or of the enemy
s.m. prop. n. Destroyer or subduer of foes.

ety: for A. عار ʻāriṉ, act. part. of عري 'to be naked,' &c.
def: adj. Naked, nude, denuded (of), bare ( = ʻuryān); destitute (of); void (of); free (from); incompetent, incapable (of, or in respect of, -meñ); distressed, distracted, helpless (by reason of, or in consequence of, -se); tired, or weary (of)
subs: or ʻārī honā (-se), To be distressed (by), to be helpless (in respect of), to be tired or sick (of), &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عاری ('ārī), from Arabic عَارِي (ʕārī).

def: s.m. Knowing, wise, sagacious, ingenious; skilled in divine matters, possessing knowledge of God and of his kingdom and of the way of dealing well with him; pious, devout; — one skilled in divine things; a holy man, a saint.

ety: ʻārif+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Wise; pious; — wisely, sagaciously, acutely; piously, devoutly.

adj. Not wounded; unhurt, unharmed, — s.m. A heron; a crow; a portent, calamity, mistortune; good fortune; a mortal disease; a sign or symptom of approaching death; the nīm-tree (Melia azadirachta); garlic; a woman's lying-in chamber.
= A ارسط و arist̤ū, (Corr. from the Greek), prop. n. The philosopher Aristotle.
= A ارسطاطاليس arist̤āt̤ālīs, (Corr. from the Greek), prop. n. The philosopher Aristotle.
s.f. Bad manners, ill-breeding, impoliteness, incivility.
s.m. (fem. aritā), Enmity, hostility.
s.m. A plant of the gourd kind.

ety: for A. عاريّة ʻārīyat, rel. n. fr. عارة ʻārat, fr. ěʻārat, 'lending,' inf. n. of iv. of عور 'to take away'
def: s.f. Lending, or borrowing, a thing (which is itself to be returned); loan (of a thing; — syn. udhār, māṅgā; — ʻārīyat differs from qarẓ, inasmuch as in the latter term it is not understood that the identical article borrowed is returned, but simply its equivalent)
subs: v.t. To take on loan, to borrow (for temporary use).

ety: for A. عاريةً, acc. of ʻārīyat
def: adv. Borrowed, on loan (syn. māṅge)
subs: (-ko), To give on loan or for use, to lend, to allow the use of.

def: adj. Borrowed; lent; — factitious, not intrinsic, not one's own, false (as hair, &c.).

ety: act. part. of عرض 'to be broad'; 'to appear,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Appearing, showing or presenting itself, happening, befalling, occurring; intervening, preventing, barring; — an occurrence, accident, casualty; an obstacle, impediment, bar; — the side of the face, the cheek; — reviewer of an army or of a body of soldiers, a muster-master; general of an army
subs: s.m. Particulars of receipts or revenues; record of occurrences or accidents; casualty-list
v.n. To show or present itself, to happen, befall (as a fever, &c.); to prove an obstacle (to), to prevent, bar, debar, &c.

def: adj. Broad, wide, expanded, large.

ety: for A. عريضة, fem. of عريض
def: s.m. Humble petition, letter (from an inferior).

ety: for A. عارضة, fem. of ʻāriẓ, q.v.
def: s.m. An accident, event, &c.; an obstacle, impediment, &c. ( = ʻāriẓ); a fracture; an affection, a disorder, disease, sickness; infirmity, defect; — anything necessary to be done; a want, an object of need (that has occurred to one).
adj. Accidental, not inherent; adventitious, casual.
wety: From Arabic عارضي (ʕāriḍiyy).

def: (for A. عارضية, fem. of ʻāriẓī), adj. f. = ʻāriẓī, q.v. adj. Accidental, not inherent; adventitious, casual.

s.f. Worth, value; respect, honour, dignity: — arj-mand [Z. mañṭ; S. mant]. Possessing worth or dignity; noble, illustrious, excellent; worthy, honourable; wise, learned; beloved, dear, precious; unequalled; rare; blessed, happy.
s.f. A Jain woman who leads the life of an ascetic.
s.m. Rectitude, honesty; sincerity, candour.

s.m. A horse having the lower part of one alone of his fore-legs white (or, according to others, any one alone of his legs white, — which is regarded in the East as a serious blemish); — a large-footed man.
s.m. Act of acquiring; acquisition; gain, earnings; profits; amassing, accumulating.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अर्जुन (árjuna).

def: = آراجاري ārā-jārī, q.v. s.f. Coming and going; ingress and egress.
v.t. caus. of next.
part. Acquired, obtained, earned, gained
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अर्जित (arjita).

def: v.s.v. ارج arj.
wety: From Classical Persian ارجمند (arjumand, “precious, valuable”)

def: v.t. To acquire, obtain, earn, gain.
s.m. Name of a hero in the Mahā, bhārat, the third of the Pānḍav princes; — a certain tree, Terminalia alata globra.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अर्जुन (árjuna).

def: s.m. The sun; a ray of light; crystal; copper; the plant Calotropis gigantea; swallow-wort, Asclepias gigantea
subs: (i.e. arka+anśa), s.m. A digit, or the twelfth part of the sun's disc
(i.e. arka+aśmana), s.m. A crystal lens; a ruby
s.m. The disc of the sun
(i.e. arka+upala), s.m. The sun-stone, a ruby; a crystal lens

def: adj. Bright, clever; sharp, acute; ingenious; strange, wonderful; new, novel. (Cf. arkaṭ.)
s.m. Lit. 'Half a rukā'; a small brass coin of the value of ten kās, or the eighth part of a Madras fanam. (Peculiar to Southern India).
s.f. pl. (of رقم raqam), Figures, numbers, &c. (See raqam).
s.m. Jaundice; mildew or blight (in corn).
s.m. pl. (of رکن rukn), Supports, props; posts, pillars, columns; component parts; fundamentals, essentials; (in Prosody) feet
subs: s.m. Lit. 'Pillars of State'; grandees, nobles; ministers of state.

def: For all comp. of which ark- is the first member, v.s.v. ارك अर्क ark.
v.s.v. اڙ aṛ.
s.m. Arcot, a city in the Deccan (Dakkhan).
s.f. Brightness, intelligence; cleverness, acuteness, ingenuity; activity; carefulness, caution, vigilance; circumspection. (Syn. surt; sāvadhānī).
adj. & s.m. Of or pertaining to Arcot; — a native or inhabitant of Arcot; a pilot (the pilots of Southern India in former times are supposed to have been men of Arcot)
s.m. Present; rarity, curiosity; a piece of money.
s.m. Wish, desire, inclination; longing; eagerness; hope; — regret, grief, sorrow; vexation; contrition, remorse; anguish of repentance
subs: v.n. To be disappointed of one's wish or hope; to have one's hope or desire ungratified
v.t. To satisfy one's longing, gratify one's wishes, enjoy the fulfilment of one's wishes or hopes
v.n. To be satisfied, or gratified.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ارمان (armān).

def: prop. n. Armenia.
adj. Desirous; hopeful; aspiring.
adj. & s.m. Armenian; an Armenian.
adj. & s.m. Armenian; an Armenian.
s.m. (f. -ī), A wild buffalo
subs: s.m. Idem; (Met.) A fat, lubberly man
s.m. Dried cowdung gathered in the jungles (used as fuel by the natives of India, and in the preparation of medicines).

def: v.n. = اڙنا aṛnā, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), A wild buffalo
subs: s.m. Idem; (Met.) A fat, lubberly man
s.m. Dried cowdung gathered in the jungles (used as fuel by the natives of India, and in the preparation of medicines).

def: v.n. To come to a stop or stand-still, to stop, to stick; to be restive; obstructive, &c., to oppose; wrangle, contend; to be obstinate, to gib (as a horse); to intervene, interpose, interfere (in, -meṅ); to come into collision, become interlocked (as the wheels of vehicles); to knock or jostle (against); to stick (at), insist (upon, -par); to be determined or bent (on); to be in pawn; to catch (at, -par), carp (at)
subs: aṛ-ke baiṭhnā, To stick, or sit, close (to); to plant oneself before a person's door in order to exact compliance with a demand; to beset importunately
wety: Urdu verb set اُڑْنا (uṛnā) اُڑانا (uṛānā) Inherited from Old Hindi उडना (uḍanā), from Prakrit 𑀉𑀟𑁆𑀟𑁂𑀇 (uḍḍei), from Sanskrit उड्डयते (uḍḍayate).

def: v.t. To stop; to support, prop; to shelter, protect.
s.m. Hare; rabbit.
s.m. The ocean, the sea.
s.f. Female wild buffalo (see arnā); — a reed of which pipes are made.
s.m. Belch, eructation.
adj. & s.m. Free from disease, sound, healthy, well; a healthy person; — freedom from sickness, soundness, healthiness, health.
adj. = اروگ arog.
adj. & s.m. = اروگ arog
subs: s.m. A restorative, anything producing health.

def: s.f. Healthiness, soundness.
adj. (f.-vatī), Health-producing, healing.
s.m. Rising, ascending, climbing, mounting; growing of new shoots, or of plants; stair-case, ladder

r: arond, araund, arondh, arondhnā, araund-se honā, kapṛoṅ-se honā
def: (for arondh), v.n. of arondhnā, q.v.
subs: To have the menses ( = kapṛoṅ-se honā).

def: v.t. To strangle, kill by stopping the breath; to throttle.
s.m. Placing in or on; assigning, applying to; attributing to; predication; change, disguise, transformation, metamorphosis; transmutation.
s.m. Placing or fixing on; causing to mount or ascend; planting; trusting, delivering; stringing (a bow).
part. Planted; strung (as a bow); deposited, entrusted; changed into, transformed, disguised; feigned.
v.t. To plant.
(see paṛosī), s. com. gen. Neighbours.
s.m. Neighbourhood; vicinity; — adv. Near; around; about.
s.m. Delivering, entrusting, placing in or upon; presenting an offering; an offering, sacrifice; a gift; a sum set apart for religious or charitable purposes
subs: v.t. To present an offering, to offer sacrifice, make an oblation; to give alms; to devote (to), set apart (for), consecrate, dedicate; to offer, present, give, consign
s.m. A deed of gift, especially to a temple or idol.

def: adv. Through and through, right through; pierced through; across; from one side to the other (also wār-pār)
subs: v.t. To pass through and through, to pierce through, penetrate.

def: part. Appointed, fixed; arranged, placed, deposited; delivered, consigned; presented.

v.t. To present an offering, &c. = arpaṇ karnā, q.v.s.v. arpaṇ. s.m. Delivering, entrusting, placing in or upon; presenting an offering; an offering, sacrifice; a gift; a sum set apart for religious or charitable purposes
subs: v.t. To present an offering, to offer sacrifice, make an oblation; to give alms; to devote (to), set apart (for), consecrate, dedicate; to offer, present, give, consign
s.m. A deed of gift, especially to a temple or idol.

def: s.m. A saw = آرا ārā, q.v.
v.n. To produce a prolonged rumbling sound (as a mill, fall of a building, &c.).
v.n. To make a loud and continued noise or sound; to lament aloud; to cry, shriek, scream; to make a noise ( = ćillānā)
subs: (-meṅ), To come rushing in; to thrust onself in, force one's way
adv. With a noise, crash, roar, or din; tumultuously; impetuously.
ex: اس کا پورا سافٹویر ہی اُڑا دیا گیا ہے۔
is kā pūrā sāfṭver hī uṛā diya gayā hai.
The OS has been completely wiped from this (machine).
wety: Compare Punjabi اُڈاݨا (uḍāṇā).

ety: from arrānā, q.v., — ṭā = S. त्व
def: s.m. A loud and prolonged sound (as from the discharge of artillery, or the fall of a building, &c.).
s.m. Hemorrhoids, piles.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अर्शस् (arśas).

ety: for A. عرصة, v.n. fr. عرص 'to play, sport,' &c.
def: s.m. Court, open area (of a house, — the 'play-ground' of children), an area; a plain; a chess-board; a space (of place or time), period, time, duration, term; an interval, a while; delay
subs: v.n. 'Space or room to be scanty'; to be in a strait, or in difficulties, to be hard pressed
v.n. To take a long time; to delay
In the interval, in the mean time.

def: s.f. = السٿا alsaṭā, q.v. Idleness, &c. = ālas, q.v. (The forms would appear to be peculiar to Southern and Western India.)
s.m. Debit and credit account, cash account.
adj. Sixty-eight.

s.m. A monthly account of receipts and disbursements ( = jamaʻ-ḵẖarć), cash account; debit and credit; guess, conjecture; valuation, appraisement; rough estimate or calculation ( = aḍsaṭṭā); a mediator, broker
subs: s.m. The clerk who keeps the cash account, &c.

ety: inf. n. of عرش 'to construct a house,' &c.
def: s.m. A roof; a canopy; the highest (the ninth) sphere, the empyrean (where the throne of God is); a throne, chair of state
subs: v.n. To be very self-conceited; to be proud or haughty
(-ko), To raise (one) to the dais, to seat one in the chair of state; to raise (one) to a high seat or rank, to exalt; to laud or praise to the skies
or ʻarsh-se-le farsh-tak, From the highest heaven to the earth
or ʻarsh-ě-barīn, s.m. The highest heaven, the empyrean.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عرش ('arš), from Arabic عَرْش (ʕarš)۔

def: s.m. One who explains orders, commands, &c. (especially of the Deity).
s.f. Mirror, looking-glass; a small mirror worn, in place of a stone, in a thumb-ring by Indian women, also the ring with the mirror.

ety: by met. for rasīlā = S. रस+इल
def: adj. Watery, moist; weak, (as in the expression) arsīle-nainā, adj. Having weak eyes; with eyes as if just awake.
s.m. A lion; a man's surname.
s.m. Touching; touch, contact; partial bathing, throwing a little water on the head with one's hand; sprinkling, aspersion; defilement or impurity arising from contact (with anything unclean)
subs: (or aras-paras) honā or ho-jānā (-se), To be touched (by) and thus rendered impure, to be defiled or polluted (by).

def: s.m. A certain ceremony in connection with marriage among Hindūs. When the bridegroom first comes to the house of the bride he is received by her relatives, who present to him, moving it circularly round his head, a platter painted and divided into several compartments filled with rice and powder for tilak, and in the middle of which is a lamp made of flour, filled with ghī, and having several wicks lighted.
part. Afflicted, pained, distressed, grieved; disturbed; injured, hurt
subs: s.m. The complaint or cry of the afflicted.

def: s.m. pl. (of رطل rat̤l), Certain measures of weight, pounds.
s.m. Patron, protector.
s.m. Shelter, defence, protection; screen, covert; shade, shadow; veil, mask; pretence, pretext
subs: (-kā), To seek or seize the shelter (of); to hide or screen oneself (behind); to shelter oneself under a false excuse; to make a pretence (of), to pretend.

def: adj. Thirty-eight.
wety: From a corruption of Apabhramsa अठतालिस (aṭhatālisa) in which the aspirated unvoiced stop ठ (ṭha) was deaspirated and voiced as ड (ḍa). Further from Prakrit 𑀅𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀢𑀸𑀮𑀻𑀲 (aṭṭhatālīsa), from Sanskrit अष्टाचत्वारिंशत् (aṣṭā́catvāriṃśat). More information Compare Punjabi ਅਠਤਾਲ਼ੀ (aṭhatāḷī) / اَٹھتاࣇِیہہ (aṭhtāḷīh), which retained the earlier Apabhramsa ठ (ṭha) between अ (a) and त (ta). Also compare Gujarati અડત…

def: s.m. Object, aim, purpose, intent; cause, motive, reason, account, sake; interest; advantage, profit, benefit, use, utility; concern, business, affair, matter; thing; substance, property, wealth, opulence; worldly prosperity; substance, material, stuff; suit, case, petition; sense, meaning, purport, import; — postpos. For, for the sake (of)
subs: (i.e. artha+antara), s.m. Another meaning; a variety of signification; another interpretation
To give the meaning (of), to explain
To apprehend the sense or meaning (of), to understand; to interpret
adj. Conveying the real import; significant; — s.m. (in Gram.) A significant term; any word which conveys a sense as distinguished from the mere unmeaning root
s.m. Distinction, difference of meaning; distinguishing between words
arth rakhnā, v.n. To have or yield the meaning (of, -kā); to mean, signify, denote; to express
s.f. Accomplishment of one's purpose, success
s.m. Institutes of the science of what is useful (e.g. friends, money, &c.)
s.m. Acquisition of wealth; attainment of one's object; appreciation of a meaning or signification
adj. & s.m. Greedy of gain; name of a jackal in one of the fables of the Hitopadesh

def: adv. In fact; that is to say, id est; namely, to wit, videlicet.

ety: by metathesis for रथ्थी or रथी = S. रथ+इका
def: s.f. A bier (such as is used by Hindūs): syn. بيوان bewān.
s.m. = آڙهتيا āṛhatiyā, q.v.

adj. & sm. Supplicating; desirous, craving; having an object to accomplish; self-interested; designing; wealthy; — supplicant, beggar, petitioner; applicant, suitor; plaintiff, prosecutor; a wealthy man; follower, dependent; protegée; servant; client; agent, man of business ( = arthiyā, q.v.).
s.m. Follower, dependent, protegée; a person recommended; client; broker; agent, man of business ( = aṛhatiyā, q.v.).
s.m. = آڙهتيا āṛhatiyā, q.v.
s.f. Pain, affliction, distress; intense desire.
s.f. A ceremony performed in adoration of a god by moving circularly around the head of the idol a platter containing a five-wicked burning lamp, flour and incense; a hymn of praise
subs: ārtī lenā, To perform the ceremony of ārtī.

def: adj. Thirty-eight.
s.m. = آڙهتيا āṛhatyā, q.v.
conj. Again; moreover; and; also; then.
s.m. A peach, Prunus domestica.
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *आडु (āḍu, “peach”), a wanderwort of unknown origin. See there for more. Cognate with Classical Persian آلُو (ālū), whence Urdu آلُو (ālū). Cognate to Punjabi ਆੜੂ (āṛū), Sindhi آڙو (āṛo).

ety: aṛ, rt. of aṛnā+ūʼā = S. aff. उक
def: adj. Obstinate, perverse, mulish; gibbing (horse); s.m. An obstinate person; a gibbing horse, a gibber.
s.f. Aversion, dislike; disgust, disrelish, want of appetite; sickness at stomach, nausea.
s.m. A medicinal plant of cooling properties growing in the Himalaya mountains.
s.m. The sun; dawn of day; colour of the dawn, reddish-brown colour
subs: s.m. Colour of the dawn, dark red
s.m. Ruby; aruṇoday, s.m. Early dawn, day-break.

def: s.f. The dawn; colour of the dawn, reddish-brown colour.
adj. Of the colour of the dawn (aruṇ), reddish-brown, tawny.
s.m. Stopping, checking, stopping the breath, strangling.
s.f. Arundhati, a particular star; the morning star.
v.t. To strangle, kill by stopping the breath; to throttle.
adj. Formless, shapeless; ugly, ill-formed: — s.m. Shapelessness; ugliness.
part. adj. Mounted on; (e.g. megh-ārūṛh, 'mounted on a cloud').
s.m. Name of a medicinal plant or tree, Justicia adhatoda.
s.m. Wound, sore; vital part; joint.

ety: v.n. fr. عرس 'to keep or cleave to,' &c.
def: s.f. A bride
subs: Writing in which the letters have the diacritical points marked.

def: s.m. A medicinal plant, Malabar nut, Justicia adhenatoda and ganderussa.

ety: ʻarūs, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Bride-like; like a bride.
adj. Bridal, nuptial; — s.f. A marriage feast.
s.f. A species of Arum, the root of which is used as a vegetable (Arum colocasia, or Colocasia antiquorum: — syn.: کچو kaććū; گهونيان ghūṅyāṅ).

ety: v.n. fr. عرض 'to show the breadth,' &c.
def: s.f. Versification, prosody (syn. pingal); — s.m. Mecca and Medina, with their adjacent territory.
s.m. Rice.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ارز (arz).

def: s.f. pl. (of روح rūḥ), Souls, spirits.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَرْوَاح (arwāh), from Arabic أَرْوَاح (ʔarwāḥ).

def: s.m. The first cuttings of corn, presented to the household gods.
s.m. Prop, support; buttress ( = āṛ); obstacle.
s.f. A species of mullet, the Indian mullet.
s.m. An Aryan; the Aryan languages.
adj. Of good family, respectable, venerable, honourable; — interj. My lord! Sir!
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit आर्य (ārya). Compare Persian آریا (âriyâ, “Aryan”).

ety: rt. aṛ of aṛnā+S. aff. इल
def: adj. Obstinate, perverse, headstrong, mulish; gibbing (horse) a gibber.
adv. On this side and that; right and left. (See ār-pār.)

ety: Zend arej, S. अर्ज, rt. अर्ह
def: s.m. Price, value; quantity; esteem, veneration, honour
subs: s.m. Invoice; particulars of the despatch of treasure, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ارز (arz).

def: s.f. The earth; land, region, country; a portion of land.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ارض (arz), from Arabic أَرْض (ʔarḍ), from Proto-Semitic *ʾarṣ́-.

ety: inf. n. of عرض 'to show the breadth,' 'to show, exhibit, present,' &c.
def: s.f. Presenting or representing; representation, petition, request, address; — (v.n. fr. عرض 'to be broad'), s.m. Breadth, width; (in Geog.) latitude; — a military muster, a review
subs: s.m. (In Eastern govts.) An officer who presents and reads letters and representations (to a king or great personage), an usher
s.m. Statement, or representation, of a case, memorial, petition
s.f. A written petition; a memorial; an address
(-kī, or -ko), To make representation (of), to represent, to submit, to state humbly; to report; to memorialize; to make application (for), to apply (for), to request, beg
s.f. The purport or matter of a petition, &c.; petition, request, case
wety: Borrowed from Arabic عَرْض (ʕarḍ).

def: adj. Blue, azure, cerulean; having cat's eyes
s.m. pl. (of رزق rizq), Riches, possessions.
incor. for ارذال arẕāl, q.v.
s.m. Low mean, or common people; the vulgar; the rabble.
adj. super. (of رذل raẕl), Very, or most, mean, vile, ignoble, &c.
adj. Cheap; abundant.
s.m. A kind of grain, millet, Panicum pilosum.
adv. In breadth; breadthwise, across.
s.f. Cheapness; abundance, plenty; good harvest
subs: Lit. 'To hold cheap'; to give away, present, bestow; — arzānī honā, To be granted or bestowed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ارزانی (arzānī).

def: s.m. The picture gallery and house of the celebrated painter Mānī.
adj. Relating to the earth; earthly; terrestrial; mundane.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ارضی (arzī), from Arabic أَرْضِيّ (ʔarḍiyy).

ety: ʻarẓ, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Representation, written statement, petition, letter (from an inferior)
subs: s.m. Petition of plaint; the first pleading in a suit
v.n. To present or file a petition; to bring an action (against)
or ʻarẓī denā, or ʻarẓī gardānnā, or ʻarẓī lagānā (-ko), To present a petition, to file a plaint
(-kī), To receive a petition; to entertain a case
s.m. One who writes petitions for suitors, a scrivener, a notary.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عرضی ('arzī), ultimately from Arabic عَرْض (ʕarḍ).

def: adj. Of breadth, pertaining to breadth or width; transverse; across, athwart (syn. āṛā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عرضی ('arzī), ultimately from Arabic عَرْض (ʕarḍ).

def: s.f. Tin; base or light money.

ety: prob. āz-ūr = āz-war, transposed; see āz
def: s.f. Wish, desire, longing, eagerness; hope; trust; expectation; intention, purpose, object, design. inclination, affection, love
subs: (caus. of next), v.t. To make one wish; to make one expect; to demand or require great eagerness, &c.
(kī), To wish, desire; long for, yearn (after); to want, need
adj. Desirous; solicitous: longing for, craving, auxious; eager; intent on
s.f. Eager desire, wishfulness; solicitude; anxiety.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آرزو (ārzō) with the majhūl vowel lost (ō > ū).

def: s.f. Wish, desire, longing; hope, expectation, prospect; trust, reliance; hope or prospect of offspring; children, offspring
subs: as pūran karnā or kar-denā (-kī), To gratify (one's) desire or longing; to fulfil the hope (of), answer the prayer (of)
ās pūrnā, ās purī honā (-kī), To be gratified (one's desire); to be fulfilled or realized (one's hope, expectation, &c.)
ās ćḥoṛnā or ćhoṛ-denā (-kī), To give up, abandon or relinquish hope
(-kī), ās lagānā (-se), To look hopefully (to); to hope for or expect (from); to trust (to), depend (upon)
ās ćhūṭnā, v.n. To lose hope, become despondent, to despond; to yield to despair
ās karnā (-kī), To entertain or cherish hope (of); to hope; to desire; to look to (for), to expect (from); to repose trust (in), place reliance (on); to feel confidence or security; to presume
v.n. To have hope or trust, be hopeful; to look for; to be expecting.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀲𑁄 (eso), from Sanskrit एषः (eṣaḥ).

def: s.f. Wish, desire, &c. = آسا āsā, q.v.
subs: s.m. Confidence, trust, expectation; a spider's web
adj. Possessed of a hoped-for object; — s.m. One who has become so possessed, a successful man
adj. Without hope, without expectation; hopeless
adj. Hopeless, destitute of hope, despondent, despairing.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit आशा (āśā). Doublet of آس (ās).

def: s.m. Staff, stick, rod, club, mace, sceptre
subs: s.m. Royal insignia; — the mace, flags, &c. borne in a marriage procession.

def: s.f. Wish, desire, longing; hope, expectation, prospect; trust, reliance; hope or prospect of offspring; children, offspring
subs: as pūran karnā or kar-denā (-kī), To gratify (one's) desire or longing; to fulfil the hope (of), answer the prayer (of)
ās pūrnā, ās purī honā (-kī), To be gratified (one's desire); to be fulfilled or realized (one's hope, expectation, &c.)
ās ćḥoṛnā or ćhoṛ-denā (-kī), To give up, abandon or relinquish hope
(-kī), ās lagānā (-se), To look hopefully (to); to hope for or expect (from); to trust (to), depend (upon)
ās ćhūṭnā, v.n. To lose hope, become despondent, to despond; to yield to despair
ās karnā (-kī), To entertain or cherish hope (of); to hope; to desire; to look to (for), to expect (from); to repose trust (in), place reliance (on); to feel confidence or security; to presume
v.n. To have hope or trust, be hopeful; to look for; to be expecting.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀲𑀸 (āsā), from Sanskrit आशा (āśā). Doublet of آشا (āśā).

def: s.m. (local) Muffler, head-covering (of a woman); a handkerchief tied round the head when going to bed.
part. adj. (used in comp.), Soothing, tranquillizing.

ety: = Hindī सा sā; used in comp.
def: adj. Like, resembling.
s.f. An unlucky moment.
s.m. A sinew, tendon, nerve, muscle; a ligament.
s.m. pl. (of عصب ʻaṣab), Nerves, tendons, sinews.
adj. Unfit for society, uncivilized, barbarous, rude, vulgar, low, disreputable.
s.f. Rudeness, unpoliteness, barbarity.
s.m. pl. (of اسبوع usbūʻ), Weeks.

ety: for A. عصبية, abst. s. fr. ʻaṣab
def: s.f. Muscularity, the state or condition of having many sinews or tendons, &c.; — a strong attachment or bond of union (holding several persons closely united); party-spirit, partisanship; esprit de corps.
= استيه asatya, and است asat, q.v. s.m. One who speaks falsehood, a liar.
s.m. Lion (syn. siṅh); Leo (the sign of the Zodiac).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسَد (asad), Arabic أَسَد (ʔasad).

def: adj. compar. & superl. (of سعيد saʻīd), More, most, or very happy or fortunate.
s.f. Incurable (chronic) complaint, mortal disease, fatal illness.
adj. Immoral, unrighteous, wicked, evil-minded; thievish.
adj. Without desire or energy; apathetic, listless; lazy.
adj. Unable, incapable; weak, feeble; confined to bed, dangerously ill; incurable; impracticable, impossible.
s.m. Wickedness, evil-mindedness; thievishness.
s.m. Lack of energy; apathy, listlessness; laziness.
s.f. = S اسادهتو असाधुत्व asādhutwa, s.m. Immorality, unrighteousness, wickedness. (See asādhpan.)
s.m. = S اسادهتا असाधुता asādhutā, s.f. Immorality, unrighteousness, wickedness. (See asādhpan.)
adj. Unable, incapable; weak, feeble; confined to bed, dangerously ill; incurable; impracticable, impossible.
adj. Non-resembling, unlike; — s.m. Unsimilarity.
s.m. The caper tree, Capparis spinosa.

r: āṣaf, Áṣaf, Áṣaf-bin-barḵẖiya, vazīr, Áṣafuʼ-d-daula, āṣaf-rāʼe
def: adj. Prudent as Áṣaf.
adj. pl. (of اصغر aṣg̠ar), Small, little; mean, contemptible; poor.
s.m. = اسگن asagun, q.v. s.m. A bad omen.
s.m. = اسگن asagun, q.v. s.m. A bad omen.
s.m. A bad omen.
adj. = اشکني aśakunī, q.v. adj. Boding ill-luck, inauspicious, portentous; — s.m. An ill-omen.
adj. Intolerable, insufferable, unbearable.
adj. Unenduring, unable to endure, not bearing with, intolerant, impatient.
adj. Unbearable, insufferable, intolerable.
s.m. Want of bravery, cowardice, fear.
adj. & s.m. Cowardly, spiritless, chicken-hearted, tame; — a coward.
adj. Without help, assistance, or companions, friendless, lonely, solitary; — s.m. One who has no assistant, helper, or companion; one who is friendless.
adj. = اسہنيه asahanīya, q.v. adj. Unbearable, insufferable, intolerable.

ety: abst. s. from āsā, rt. of āsūdan
def: s.f. Ease, rest, repose, quiet, tranquillity; convenience, comfort; indulgence, enjoyment.
s.m. pl. (of ʻuṣfūr), Sparrows.
adj. Unfit, improper; untrue; not of good family, not well-born, not respectable; not virtuous, disreputable.
= اشکت aśakta, q.v. adj. Unable, incompetent, powerless, helpless.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اشک (ašk) from Proto-Iranian *Hácru, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂éḱru (“tear”). Doublet of آن٘سُو (ā̃sū) and اشر (aśru).

def: adj. Without credit or reputation; — s.m. Want of evidence or testimony.
adj. & s.m. = اساکشي asākshī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Of inadmissible evidence or testimony; — false witness; one whose evidence or testimony is inadmissible.
s.m. pl. (of ʻaskar), Armies, forces, troops.
adj. & adv. Not manifest, invisible, imperceptible; — not openly or manifestly; not in the presence of; invisibly, unperceived; in the absence of.
adj. & s.m. Of inadmissible evidence or testimony; — false witness; one whose evidence or testimony is inadmissible.
adj. Without credit or reputation; — s.m. Want of evidence or testimony.
part. adj. Strongly attached to; fond of, enamoured of, addicted (to), devoted to; intent on, engrossed; zealously following or pursuing.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit आसक्त (āsakta).

def: adj. Unable, incompetent, powerless, helpless.
wety: From Sanskrit अशक्त (aśakta).

def: adj. = آسکت āsakt, q.v. part. adj. Strongly attached to; fond of, enamoured of, addicted (to), devoted to; intent on, engrossed; zealously following or pursuing.
s.f. Attachment, fondness, devotedness, love; zealous application, diligence.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit आसक्त (āsakta).

def: s.f. Inability, incapability, impotence, weakness, helplessness.
wety: From Sanskrit अशक्त (aśakta).

def: s.f. = آسکت āsakti, q.v. s.f. Attachment, fondness, devotedness, love; zealous application, diligence.
s.m. = اسگن asagun, q.v. s.m. A bad omen.
adj. Boding ill-luck, inauspicious, portentous; — s.m. An ill-omen.
adj. Impracticable, impossible.
s.m. Honey (syn. shahd; madhu).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَسَل (asal), from Arabic عَسَل (ʕasal).

ety: inf. n. of اصل 'to be firm'
def: s.f. Firmness, the being well-rooted; genuineness; integrity; constancy; solidity of judgment; essentiality; nobleness.
adv. Originally, radically; firmly; entirely; absolutely; in proper person, personally (opp. of wikālataṉ).
prop. n. Name of a province, Assam.
wety: Borrowed from English Assam.

def: adj. Unequal; uneven, not level; unlike, dissimilar; unusual.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْم (ism), borrowed from Arabic اِسْم (ism).

def: adj. Without understanding (samajh, q.v.); thoughtless, stupid, ignorant.
adj. Uneven, not level; unlike, different, dissimilar; unequal.
s.m. Doubt, suspense; suspicion; unconformity, disparity, difference.
adv. All round, on every side; wholly, altogether.
adj. Not common, not usual; specific, particular, peculiar.
part. adj. Unfinished, incomplete.
s.f. Incompleteness.
adj. Powerless, weak, feeble, helpless, unable; unfit, incompetent, incapable
adj. = اسامرتهہ asāmarth, q.v. adj. Powerless, weak, feeble, helpless, unable; unfit, incompetent, incapable
s.m. A time of misfortune or distress, adverse or calamitous time or season; inconvenient or unsuitable time; — adj. Ill-timed, out of season, unseasonable; premature.
part. adj. Not joined, unconnected, disconnected.
adj. Uncommon; inconceivable, improbable; unreasonable, absurd, inconsistent, incompatible, impossible.
s.f. Inconceivableness, improbability; unreasonableness, inconsistency, incompatibility; impossibility; unexpectedness; wretchedness.
part. adj. = اسمبهو asambhav, q.v. adj. Uncommon; inconceivable, improbable; unreasonable, absurd, inconsistent, incompatible, impossible.
adj. & s.m. Of or pertaining to Assam; a native of Assam, an Assamese.
wety: From آسام (āsām, “Assam”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: pl. of اسما asmā, the pl. of اسم ism; or rel. adj. from asām, pl. of asmā, 'names'
def: s.f. Settlement with individual cultivators
subs: s.m.f. Tenant with right of occupancy
According to, or including all the names; giving a detailed account of the asāmīs; singly, separately; in detail
(or yāft-kī asāmī), s.f. Lucrative appointment; an office in which perquisites or bribes are to be had.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسَامِی (asāmī), from Arabic أَسَامٍ (ʔasāmin).

def: adj. Deaf; (in Arith.) surd (opp. of munt̤iq; e.g. jaẕr-i-aṣamm, 'the square root of a surd').
s.m. Disrespect; disgrace.
adj. Dissentient, averse; adverse, contrary.
= اسمت asammat. q.v. adj. Dissentient, averse; adverse, contrary.
s.f. Disagreement, dissent, dissension, contrariety, aversion, dislike.
adj. Incomplete, imperfect, defective.
s.m. Eating, food; — the tree Terminalia alata tormentosa, on which the ṭasar silkworm feeds (also written आसन āsan)

s.m. Sitting; sitting in peculiar postures according to the custom of Jogīs in their devotional exercises (of which eighty-four are enumerated); mode of sitting, posture, attitude, position; seat, place, stool; a small carpet or deer-skin on which Hindūs sit during prayer; the withers of an elephant, the part where the driver sits; driver's seat; rider's seat (on a horse); the inside or under part of the thigh; a market-stall; — maintaining a post against an enemy
subs: To come under the thighs, to be ridden or mounted (a horse), to come under subjection; to be embraced in carnal intercourse
v.n. To have a good grip with the thighs (on a horse), to sit firmly, stick (on)
āsan joṛke baiṭhnā, āsan-se āsan joṛnā, v.n. To sit on the hams; to squat on the ground like a Jogī; to sit in close contact (with, lit. 'thigh to thigh')
(-ke), To shake the firm-sitting or position (of), to shake the virtue (of); to allure, tempt, seduce
To move or interpose in behalf of (said of a holy man who perceives by supernatural intelligence that some one in distress has invoked his aid)
(-ke), To sit down, settle, take up (one's) abode, abide; tarry; to sit obstinately in one place and position till one's demands are satisfied
(-par), āsan mār-ke baiṭhnā (-par), To sit on the haunches (like a Jogī), to squat; to sit in one posture without change; to have a firm seat (on a horse).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit आसन (āsana).

ety: fr. āsā, rt. of āsūdan; see āsūda
def: adj. Easy, facile; smooth; light; feasible; manageable; convenient, commodious; unencumbered, disencumbered (affair)
subs: v.t. To render easy; to facilitate, expedite, smooth, lighten; to clear away, disencumber, free (from), disentangle
(-par), To be easy (to or for); to be practicable.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آسَان (āsān).

def: = S آشن आशन āśan, see اسن asan or آسن āsan (the tree so called).
= S اشن अशन aśan, see اسن asan or آسن āsan (the tree so called).
adj. = اسچ asać. q.v. s.m. One who speaks falsehood, a liar.
part. adj. Undoubted, unquestionable.
s.m. Instrumentality, means, help; — s.f. Power, ability, strength
adj. Not holding together, incoherent, incongruous, inconsonant, unreasonable, false, absurd, improper, bad.
s.f. A small carpet, deer-skin, &c. on which Hindūs sit at prayer.
s.f. Ease, facility; easiness; smoothness; practicableness, feasibility; convenience, commodiousness
subs: adv. With ease, easily, without difficulty ( = ba-asānī).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آسانی (āsānī). By surface analysis, آسَان (āsān) + ـِی (-ī).

def: = اسنيوگ asaṅyog, q.v. s.m. Want of opportunity; separation.
adj. Free from doubt or hesitation, undoubting, not anxious, confident; secure, fearless, undaunted.
s.f. Doubt, uncertainty; fear, apprehension, awe, danger
wety: From Sanskrit अ- (a-) + शङ्क (śaṅka).

def: adj. = اسنك asank, q.v. adj. Free from doubt or hesitation, undoubting, not anxious, confident; secure, fearless, undaunted.
s.f. = آسنکا āsankā, q.v. s.f. Doubt, uncertainty; fear, apprehension, awe, danger
adj. Free from doubt or hesitation, undoubting, not anxious, confident; secure, fearless, undaunted.
adj. Countless, innumerable.
part. adj. Unnumbered, not calculated or computed; numberless.
s.m. Disrespect, irreverence.
adj. Near, close, at hand, adjacent, proximate
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit आसन (āsana).

def: s.m. Absence of doubt; — adj. Undoubted.
part. adj. Unappeased; unresigned, restless, anxious, disquieted, fearful, apprehensive.
adj. Without offspring, childless.
s.f. Restlessness, anxiety.
s.m. Dissatisfaction, discontent; displeasure; unrest.
s.m. Dissatisfaction, discontent; displeasure; unrest.
adj. Unsatisfied, dissatisfied, discontented.
part. adj. Dissatisfied, discontented, displeased, aggrieved; hard to please.
s.m. Absence of self-restraint; uncontrol, incontinence, intemperateness.
adj. & s.m. Unrestrained, incontinent, intemperate; — an incontinent person.
s.m. Want of opportunity; separation.

ety: pl. of ثار, the arabicised form of सेर ser
def: s.m. Seers; seer (ser, 'a weight of two pounds').
s.m. Footprint; sign, mark, token, trace, track, vestige, shadow; impress, impression, influence; effect; result, consequence
subs: To have or take effect (on), to prove efficacious, to effect, be effective; to operate; to make an impression; to affect, move, touch; to stimulate; to excite (the passions)
An effect to be produced, to have effect (on, -meṅ); to prevail.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اثر (asar), from Arabic أَثَر (ʔaṯar).

def: adj. Sapless, pithless; hollow (as a reed); unsubstantial; unprofitable, vain; weak of understanding, foolish, silly: — s.m. Defect.

s.m. Footprints, vestiges, tracks, traces, marks, signs, tokens, remains, relics, monuments or memorials; effects; impressions; indications of state or condition, promises, symptoms; sayings or traditions of Moḥammad; origin; basis, foundation (of a building, &c.); breadth of a wall
subs: ās̤ār-i-mubārak, s.m. The venerated sayings or traditions of Moḥammad; the sacred relics of Moḥammad; the four companions or disciples of Moḥammad who were his successors.

def: adj. Without shelter, unprotected, helpless, destitute.
adj. = اسرن asaraṇ, q.v. adj. Without shelter, unprotected, helpless, destitute.
s.f. Want of solidity; vanity; unreality; unprofitableness; unenduringness; worthlessness.
s.m. The third solar month among the Hindūs (June-July), during which the sun is in Gemini. It is the first month of the rainy season, and consequently of cultivation.
s.m. The third solar month among the Hindūs (June-July), during which the sun is in Gemini. It is the first month of the rainy season, and consequently of cultivation.
s.f. Day of the full moon in asāṛh (used for the rabīʻ or spring harvest); the harvest of asāṛh.
